npcs.name: Old Gantry
npcs.title: who found the empty place
npcs.hail: *(a stooped old shade stands vigil over a patch of nothing — not a body, not a grave, just a *space* in the gathered dead where a soul plainly used to be, an absence with edges — and he turns to you with the particular terror of someone who has seen a thing the dead are not supposed to be able to see: one of their own, not moved on, not walled, but simply, wrongly, *gone*)*

npcs.name: the Bereaved
npcs.title: who loved the un-made one
npcs.hail: *(a soul kneels at the edge of the absence with both hands pressed to the empty air where someone used to be, and the grief on it is a strange, frantic, incomplete thing — because you cannot properly mourn a soul that has been un-made, there being nothing left to mourn, not even the memory of what was lost, which keeps sliding out of reach like a name on the tongue)*

npcs.name: the Quiet Neighbor
npcs.title: who has an answer you won't like
npcs.hail: *(a calm, unremarkable shade watches the inquest from a careful distance with its hands folded and its face composed, and your dead-touched sense prickles at it the way it never does at the innocent — not because the soul is cruel, but because it is the only one here who is not afraid, and in a crowd terrified of a thing that un-makes souls, the one soul that isn't frightened is the one that knows exactly what's happening, because it is the one making it happen)*

conversations.title: Old Gantry and the Empty Place

variants.text: *(He turns from the patch of nothing as you approach, and reads the certainty in your cold face, and his old shade goes very still.)* …You *know.* *(barely)* You've *been* *round*, *asked* the *questions*, and you've come *back* with the *look* of a soul that's *worked* it *out.* *(he braces himself)* Then *tell* me, traveler — *gently*, if you *can*, but *tell* me *true*: who *un-made* *Sella?* Who reached into the *gathered* *dead* and *erased* a soul so *complete* that even *grief* can't *find* her? *(his voice shakes)* I've *kept* this *vigil* *three* *nights.* I'm *ready* for the *answer.* I have to *be.* *Someone* has to *know* what *happened* in this *place*, or it just — *happens* *again*, to the *next* one, in the *quiet.*

*(He clutches at your sleeve the instant your cold reaches him, the relief of the terrified at any soul that doesn't flinch.)* …You're *cold.* *Proper* cold — the *Returned* kind, the *whispered* kind. *(he pulls you toward the patch of nothing)* Then maybe *you* can *see* it. *Look.* *Look* there — no, don't look *for* something, look for the *space* where something *isn't.* *(and you do, and there it is: an absence with edges, a soul-shaped hole in the gathered dead)* …Her *name* was *Sella.* I *think.* It's *going* — even *that's* *going* — but I'm *old* and *stubborn* and I'm *holding* onto the *one* *syllable* I can. *Sella.* *(he meets your eyes, frantic)* She wasn't *walled*, traveler. She wasn't *moved* *on.* She was *UN-MADE* — *erased*, *root* and *memory*, which the *dead* are *not* *supposed* to be *able* to *do* to each *other.* *Something* down here *can.* And I am *begging* you — *find* out *what.* Before the *space* in the *dead* gets *another* set of *edges.*

nodes.speaker: Old Gantry
nodes.text: *(He stands guard over the absence, an old soul refusing to let a hole go un-witnessed.)* Ask me what you *need*, traveler. *(grimly)* I *found* it. I'll *answer* for what I *found.* Then *go* — *ask* the *others.* There's a soul that *loved* her, and a soul that *watches* too *calm.* *Between* them, and *this* empty place, the *truth's* in here *somewhere.* I'd *stake* my *last* *syllable* of her *name* on it.

choices.text: "Tell me what an un-making even is, Gantry. The dead get walled, or move on — how is 'erased' different, and how do you know that's what happened?"
"Who was here, the night it happened? Give me everyone near this place — the grieving, the calm one, anyone."

*(let the dead-touched read the absence itself)* "Stand back, Gantry. Let me read the empty place — the way only my kind can. An un-making leaves a residue; nothing is ever truly nothing. There'll be a shape to how she was taken, and a shape tells you the hand. Give me a moment with the hole where Sella was."
*(if you've gathered both clues)* "I've asked the others, Gantry. I know who did it — and I know why, which is worse. Let me lay it out."
"Hold your vigil, Gantry. I'll go ask around."

nodes.speaker: Old Gantry
nodes.text: *(He shudders, and the words come slow, a man describing a thing the mind keeps trying to slide away from.)* …What an *un-making* *is.* *(quietly)* When a soul gets *walled*, traveler, it's *cruel*, but it's — it's *somewhere.* In the *stone.* *Suffering*, but *extant.* When a soul *moves* *on*, it *goes* — *somewhere* *else*, through the *good* door, and you *feel* the *going*, a *warmth*, a *leaving.* *(he gestures at the absence)* But *this.* *This* is *neither.* No *stone* *took* her. No *door* *opened.* She is not *somewhere* *suffering* and she is not *somewhere* *resting.* She is *NOT.* *(his voice cracks)* And the *proof* — the *thing* that *tells* me it's an *un-making* and not just a *moving-on* I *missed* — is the *grief.* When a soul *moves* *on*, those who *loved* it *grieve* it *clean*: they *remember*, they *ache*, they *heal.* *(he points at the kneeling Bereaved)* But *look* at *that* soul. It *can't* *grieve* *right*, because the *un-making* *took* the *memory* *too* — it's *trying* to *mourn* someone it can no longer *quite* *recall*, *clutching* at a *name* that *won't* *stay.* *(grimly)* *That's* an *un-making*, traveler. Not just a *death.* A *theft* of the *having-existed.* And *that*, the *dead* *cannot* *do* to each *other.* So *something* down here is *more* than *dead.*

nodes.speaker: Old Gantry
nodes.text: *(He counts them off on translucent fingers.)* …Who was *near.* *(grimly)* *Two* souls, traveler, besides *me.* *(he nods at the kneeling shade)* *That* one — the *Bereaved.* *Loved* *Sella*, *clearly*, the way it *claws* at the *empty* *air.* Been *here* since *before* I *found* the *hole*, *weeping* over a *space.* *(he frowns)* *Suspicious*, you'd *think* — the *one* who *loved* her, *first* on the *scene.* But *grief* like *that* you *can't* *fake*, and *what's* the *motive*, *un-making* the *thing* you *love?* *(he turns to the calm shade)* And *that* one. The *Quiet* *Neighbor.* *(his voice drops)* I *don't* — I *can't* say *why*, traveler, but it's the *one* that *frightens* me. *Everyone* in this place is *terrified* — a soul got *un-MADE*, we're *all* *next*, the *fear's* *thick* as *river-fog.* *Everyone* but *that* one. *Calm.* *Folded* *hands.* *Watching.* *(he meets your eyes)* My *old* *bones* say the soul that *isn't* *afraid* of the *thing* *eating* *souls* is the *soul* that *knows* it *won't* be *eaten.* And *why* *wouldn't* it be *afraid* it's *next* — *unless* it *knows* who's *doing* the *eating?* *(grimly)* *Ask* them *both.* But *mind* the *calm* one. *Calm's* the *tell.*

nodes.speaker: Old Gantry
nodes.text: *(He steps back, and you turn your dead-touched sense full on the soul-shaped hole — and where the living would find only absence, your cold reads the *grain* of it, the direction the un-making pulled.)* *(He watches your face change.)* …What. *(barely)* What do you *see?* *(you tell him: that the absence isn't ragged, the way a *violent* erasure would tear — it's *clean*, *deliberate*, *fed* somewhere, the soul not *destroyed* but *channeled*, drained in a single careful direction like water down a *cut* drain — and at the lip of the hole, the faintest residue of a *second* cold, a soul that did the draining and walked away *fuller.*)* *(Gantry goes grey.)* …*Fed.* *Channeled.* *(he understands)* Not *destroyed* — *eaten.* By a *soul* that *walked* *away* *fuller.* *(he grips your arm)* Then it's not a *force*, traveler, not a *curse* nor a *Hunger* loose on its *own.* It's a *who.* A soul *down* *here* *un-making* *others* and *taking* what they *were.* *(grimly)* You've the *direction* of it *now* — the *cold* it *left.* *Follow* it. It'll lead to the *one* soul in this *place* that's a *little* *warmer*, a *little* *fuller*, than a *dead* thing has *any* *right* to *be.*

nodes.speaker: Old Gantry
nodes.text: *(He straightens, bracing, an old soul ready at last to hear the truth he kept vigil for.)* …Say it, then. *(quietly)* Who *un-made* *Sella?* And you said — you said the *why* was *worse.* *(he steels himself)* *Tell* me the *worse* thing, traveler. I've *earned* the *whole* of it.

choices.text: "It was the Quiet Neighbor. But hear the why before you damn it: it's not eating souls for power. It found a Hunger-fragment growing under this place — a crumb of the same nothing that fills the Wall — and it discovered that feeding the fragment one soul keeps it from spreading and eating *everyone.* It un-made Sella to save the whole Underbridge. It's a soul committing murder as a mercy, and it's been right every time, which is the horror of it."

"It was the Quiet Neighbor — the calm one, the only soul here unafraid, because it's the one doing it. I don't fully know its reasons yet, but the hand is clear. The cold it left at the edge of the absence is the cold it carries."
"I know who. But I'd hear the Neighbor's own account before I hand you a name to hate. Let me finish with it first."

nodes.speaker: Old Gantry
nodes.text: *(He sits down slowly on the empty air, the fight going out of him, replaced by something far heavier than the simple grief he'd braced for.)* …A *mercy.* *(barely)* It *un-made* *Sella* to *save* the *rest* of *us.* *(he presses his hands to his face)* *Gods*, traveler. I came to this *vigil* *wanting* a *villain.* A *thing* to *hate*, a *wrong* to *right*, *clean.* And you've *handed* me a *soul* doing the *most* *terrible* *arithmetic* there *is* — *one* of *us*, *fed* to the *nothing*, so the *nothing* doesn't *take* *all* of us. *(he looks at the absence)* And the *worst* of it — the *thing* you *meant* by *'worse'* — is that it's *WORKING.* *Sella's* *gone*, and the *fragment's* *quiet*, and the *Underbridge* *stands.* *(his voice breaks)* How do I *hate* a soul that's been *RIGHT?* *(he looks at you, lost)* …What do we *DO*, traveler? *Stop* it, and let the *nothing* *spread* and *eat* *everyone?* *Let* it *continue*, and *become* the *kind* of *soul* that *files* its *neighbors* into the *dark*, one a *month*, *forever?* *(quietly)* This is *why* the *Wall* *exists*, isn't it. *Someone*, *somewhere*, did this *exact* *arithmetic* on a *cosmic* *scale* and *called* it a *Concord.* *(he meets your eyes)* You've *seen* the *whole* causeway. You *tell* me. Is there a *third* *answer? Or is it always, only, ever this?*

nodes.speaker: Old Gantry
nodes.text: *(He turns toward the calm, folded-handed shade, and his old face hardens into something almost like resolve.)* …The *Quiet* one. *(grimly)* I *knew* it. The *calm.* The *tell.* *(he rises)* *Thank* you, traveler. For *naming* it. A *thing* with a *name* can be *faced* — it's the *nameless* *holes* in the *dead* that *break* a soul. *(but he hesitates)* …You said you *don't* *fully* *know* its *reasons.* *(he frowns)* *Mind* that, traveler, before *I* go and *do* something *I* can't *un-do.* The *causeway* taught *me* *one* *thing*, *watching* the *Choir* *wall* the *Faithless* on *certainty*: that the soul *most* *sure* it's *found* a *villain* is the soul *most* *likely* to *make* a *fresh* one. *(grimly)* *Go* *back.* *Finish* it. Learn the *why* before we settle the *what.* I've *waited* *three* *nights.* I can *wait* for the *whole* *truth.* I'd *rather* face a *monster* I *understand* than *make* one I *don't.*

nodes.speaker: Old Gantry
nodes.text: *(He nods, and returns his grey gaze to the soul-shaped hole, an old shade refusing to let an absence go unwatched.)* …*Ask* around. *(quietly)* I'll *keep* the *place.* *Someone* *has* to. *(he gestures at the empty air)* You know the *strangest* thing, traveler? *Three* nights I've *stood* here, and *not* *one* of the *passing* dead will *look* at it. The *hole.* They *walk* *round* it, *eyes* *down*, like if they *don't* *see* the *space* where *Sella* *was*, it *can't* *happen* to *them.* *(grimly)* But it's the *not-looking* that *feeds* it, isn't it. *Whatever* *it* *is.* The *not-looking* is how a *soul* gets *forgotten* *enough* to *vanish* in the *first* *place.* *(he meets your eyes)* So *that's* my *vigil*, traveler. Not *guarding* the *hole.* *Looking* at it. *Refusing* to *walk* *round.* It's the *one* thing an *old* shade can still *do* — *witness* the *empty* *place*, so the *empty* *place* is *witnessed.* *Go* on. *Find* the *truth.* I'll be *here*, *looking*, till you *bring* it *back.*

nodes.speaker: Old Gantry
nodes.text: *(He nods, slowly, and the respect in his old eyes deepens.)* …You'd *hear* it *out.* *(quietly)* Before you *hand* an old soul a *name* to *hate.* *(he settles back to his vigil)* That's — that's the *right* of it, traveler. I *wanted* a *villain* *handed* to me *quick*, so the *fear* would have *somewhere* to *go.* But a *quick* *villain's* how the *Choir* *works*, and I'll *not* run my *grief* the way *they* run their *Wall.* *(he gestures you back toward the calm shade)* *Go.* *Hear* the *Quiet* one's *whole* account. *Then* come *tell* me — not *who*, I think I *know* the *who* — but *what* *we* *do* about it. *That's* the *harder* *question*, and the *only* one *worth* a *three-night* *vigil.*

variants.text: *(The kneeling soul doesn't look up from the empty air, and its voice is the cracked, circling tone of grief that can't find its object.)* …I'm trying to *remember* her *face.* *(barely)* I *had* it. This *morning* I *had* it — the *eyes*, the — *(it falters)* — there was a *thing* she *did*, with her *mouth*, when she was *about* to *laugh*, and I — *(its hands clutch the empty air)* …it's *going*, traveler. *She's* going *out* of *me* the way she went out of the *world*, and I can't — you can't *hold* a soul that's being *un-made*, there's *nothing* to *hold*, the *un-making* *reaches* into *everyone* who *knew* her and *takes* her *there* too. *(it finally looks at you, desperate)* You're *cold.* You *see* the *dead* *clear.* *Tell* me her *face*, traveler. *Please.* Before I lose the *last* of — before *I'm* the *last* place she *exists* and then I'm *not* either. *Tell* me *something* of *Sella* I can *keep.*

nodes.speaker: the Bereaved
nodes.text: *(It kneels at the absence, clutching after a memory like water, and turns its streaming face to you.)* Ask me — *(it falters)* — ask me *quick*, while there's *still* a *me* that *knew* her to *ask.* *(barely)* I don't know how *much* longer I'll *have* the *answers.*

choices.text: "Who was Sella to you? Tell me while you can — saying it aloud to me might keep it, even as it fades from you."
"Did you see anyone near her, before she was gone? Anyone who shouldn't have been? Think — even a small thing."

*(let the dead-touched hold the memory the un-making is stealing)* "Look at me. I'm the one kind of soul that can hold what the un-making is taking — I carry the dead the living can't. Tell me her face, the laugh, the thing with her mouth, all of it, and I'll keep it in the cold where the erasure can't reach. You won't lose her alone. Some of her will live in me, witnessed, safe, no matter what took her."
"I'll find what took her, friend. Hold on to what you can."

nodes.speaker: the Bereaved
nodes.text: *(It closes its eyes, reaching, and the words come in a rush, as if speed could outrun the forgetting.)* …She was — *quick.* *(grasping)* Quick to *laugh*, quick to *help*, quick to — she *fed* the *new* dead, the *frightened* ones who *come* down *not* *knowing* they've *died.* *Sella* *sat* with them. *Explained* it *gentle.* *(a cracked breath)* *Forty* *years* she did it, before *she* died, a *midwife's* — no, that's — *(it falters)* — was she a *midwife? I* had it. *(it presses on)* She had a *way* of — when a *soul* came *down* *screaming*, *terrified*, *new*, she'd take its *hands* and say *'there now, there now, you're only dead, it's the gentlest thing that ever happened to you, let me show you'* — *(it weeps)* — and they'd *calm.* *Every* time. She *un-frightened* the *dead*, traveler. That's what she *did.* That's *who* she *was.* *(it looks at you, anguished)* And *something* *un-MADE* her. The *one* soul down here who made the *after* *less* *terrible* — *gone*, *root* and *memory*, while the *cruel* ones *stay.* *(barely)* *Why* her? Why *always* the *gentle* ones? *Keep* that, traveler — *'she un-frightened the dead.'* If you keep *nothing* *else* of *Sella*, keep *that.*

nodes.speaker: the Bereaved
nodes.text: *(It frowns, dredging through fog.)* …Someone *near* her. *(slowly)* The night before the — before the *space.* *(its brow furrows)* The *Quiet* one. The *Neighbor.* *(it looks toward the calm shade)* I *saw* them *talking.* *Sella* and the *Quiet* one. And I didn't *think* — *Sella* talked to *everyone*, that was *Sella* — but the *Quiet* one was — *(it strains)* — it was *crying.* *(surprised by the memory)* The *Quiet* one was *crying*, and *Sella* had its *hands*, doing the *thing* she *did*, the *'there now'* — *un-frightening* it. *(its eyes widen)* And the *next* *morning*, *Sella* was a *space*, and the *Quiet* one was — *calm.* *Dry-eyed.* *Settled.* *(a chill goes through it)* …I thought it was just *grief* *passing.* But you don't *un-make* a soul by *accident*, do you, traveler. And the *last* thing *Sella* ever did was *comfort* the *very* soul that — *(it can't finish)* — *go.* *Ask* the *Quiet* one why it was *crying.* And why it *stopped.*

nodes.speaker: the Bereaved
nodes.text: *(It seizes your cold hands, and there's a desperate hope in it now, and it pours out everything it has left of her — fast, complete, before it goes — and you take it, into the cold, where you keep the dead the living can't.)* *(When it finishes, it is somehow lighter, and emptier, and grateful.)* …You *have* her. *(barely)* I can *feel* it — you've *taken* her into the *cold*, the *face*, the *laugh*, the *'there now'*, all of it — and even as it goes *out* of *me*, I *know* it's *somewhere*, *held*, *witnessed*, *safe* from the *un-making.* *(it weeps, but the weeping is clean now)* I'm *losing* her, traveler. I'll be *empty* of her by *dawn* — the *erasure's* in me *too*, there's *no* *stopping* that. But she won't be *GONE.* She'll be in *you.* *(it looks at you with profound gratitude)* That's — that's the *thing* the *un-making* *couldn't* *count* on, isn't it. A soul that can *carry* the *erased* *out* of *reach.* *(fierce, through tears)* *Go* *find* what *took* her. And *when* you *face* it — *tell* it *Sella's* *not* *gone.* Tell it the *one* soul it *couldn't* *erase* *her* from walked up out of the *cold* and *kept* her. *Tell* it *un-making* *doesn't* *work* *anymore.* Not *while* *you're* *here.*

nodes.speaker: the Bereaved
nodes.text: *(It nods, and turns back to the empty air, and resumes its impossible vigil over a fading absence.)* …Find it. *(barely)* And — traveler? *(it looks up)* When you *do* — when you *know* *who*, and *why* — *don't* let them tell you it was *necessary.* *(its voice hardens through the grief)* *Everyone* who does a *terrible* thing down here says it was *necessary.* The *Choir* says it. The *Wall* says it. *(it presses a hand to the absence)* *Maybe* it *was.* Maybe *Sella* *had* to *go*, for some — some *reason* I'm *too* *small* to *see.* *(fiercely)* But *necessary* or *not*, she was *SOMEONE*, and she *un-frightened* the *dead*, and the *world* is *colder* without her in it, and *that's* *true* *even* if the *un-making* was *right.* *Hold* *both*, traveler. The *reason* *and* the *loss.* *Don't* let the *reason* *eat* the *loss* the way the *un-making* *ate* *her.* That's — *(barely)* — that's all I *ask.* *Mourn* her, even if you *can't* *blame* anyone. *Someone* has to. And I'm — I'm *running* *out* of *me* to *do* it.

variants.text: *(The calm shade watches you approach with the cold residue of the absence still clinging to it, and it does not pretend, because it can see that you have read the empty place and know its hand is in it.)* …You followed the *cold.* *(quietly, without fear)* Of course you did. You're the *Returned*; *reading* what's *left* is the *whole* of what you *are.* *(it folds its hands)* So you know it was *me.* I'll not *insult* you with *denial* — *denial* is for souls who think they did *wrong.* *(it meets your eyes, calm as still water)* *Sit*, if a *thing* like *us* can *sit.* Let me tell you *why* a *quiet* *neighbor* *un-makes* the *gentlest* soul on the *Underbridge* — and let me tell you the *part* you're *dreading*, which is that I was *right* to.*

*(The calm shade inclines its head as you approach, composed where every other soul in the place is shaking, and that composure is itself the most unsettling thing you've felt since the Wall.)* …You're the soul *asking* the *questions.* *(mildly)* About the *empty* *place.* *(it does not fidget, does not flee)* I *wondered* who they'd *send.* The *dead* are *frightened*, and *frightened* *souls* *want* a *soul* who *isn't*, to *make* it make *sense.* *(it studies you)* You're *cold.* *Returned.* *Good.* The *frightened* ones would *not* *understand* what I have to *say* — they'd *only* *scream.* But *you* — you've *walked* the whole *causeway*, I'd *wager. You've* *seen* the *Wall*, the *Concord*, the *whole* *grim* *machine* of *necessary* *cruelty.* *(it meets your eyes, calm)* So *you*, of *all* souls, might *understand* the *small* *version* of it I've been *running*, *alone*, in the *dark*, under this *bridge.* *Ask*. I'll *answer* *true.* I've been *waiting* for *one* soul who *could* *hear* it.*

nodes.speaker: the Quiet Neighbor
nodes.text: *(It waits, hands folded, the only calm thing in a terrified place.)* Ask, Returned. *(quietly)* I have *carried* this *alone* for *longer* than you'd *think.* I find I am — *almost* — *relieved* to be *found.*

choices.text: "Why, then? You un-made Sella — the gentlest soul here, by every account. Tell me why, and tell me true."

[INSIGHT] "You're not enjoying this. A soul that fed on others for power would be fuller, hungrier, *worse* each time — and you just look tired. You're not a predator. You're a *warden*, aren't you. You found something down here, and you've been feeding it to keep it from feeding itself. What did you find under this bridge?"

*(the one who witnessed the cistern's Hunger, to the one feeding a new one)* "I've seen your problem before, Neighbor — a crumb of the Hunger, the nothing that fills the Wall, growing where the dead are forgotten. There was one in the cistern. We didn't feed it. We gave it back its name and witnessed it into a person. Whatever you've been feeding souls to, it isn't fed by death. It's fed by being forgotten. You've had the cure backwards this whole time."
"I've heard enough to know you're the hand. The why can wait. Don't go anywhere, Neighbor."

nodes.speaker: the Quiet Neighbor
nodes.text: *(It is quiet a long moment, and when it speaks there is no defensiveness in it, only the flat exhaustion of a soul that has done a terrible thing many times and made its peace with the doing, if not the thing.)* …Why. *(softly)* There is a *thing* under this *bridge, Returned.* In the *old* *foundations*, where the *dead* have been *forgotten* *longest.* A — *(it searches for the word)* — a *hole.* A *crumb* of the *nothing* that *fills* the *Wall.* It *grows.* *(it meets your eyes)* I *found* it *by* *accident*, a *year* *ago*, when it had *eaten* *one* *soul* — a *pauper* *no* one *remembered.* And I *watched* it, *terrified*, and I *learned* its *rule*: it *eats.* And what it *eats*, it *grows* *by.* And if it grows *enough*, it *stops* eating *one* soul at a *time* and eats the *whole* *Underbridge* at *once* — *every* soul here, *un-made*, *root* and *memory*, *gone.* *(its calm cracks, just slightly)* So I made a *bargain* *with* *myself.* I *feed* it.* *One* *soul.* *Carefully* *chosen.* *Before* it gets *hungry* enough to *take* *everyone.* And it *stays* *small*, and *quiet*, and the *Underbridge* *lives.* *(it looks at the absence)* *Sella* was the — the *fourth.* I choose the — *(its voice catches)* — I choose the *gentlest.* *Always* the *gentlest.* Because a *gentle* soul, I told myself, would *understand.* Would *want* to *save* the *others.* Would *forgive* me. *(barely)* *Sella* *did.* That's the *worst* of it. I *told* her, at the *end*, and she *took* my *hands* and said *'there now'* and *let* me *do* it. *(it meets your eyes, hollow)* I am not a *monster*, Returned. I am a soul doing the *arithmetic* of the *Wall*, *alone*, in the *dark*, and being *right*, and *hating* it, *every* *single* *time.*

nodes.speaker: the Quiet Neighbor
nodes.text: *(Something flickers in the still water of its face — the relief of the correctly seen.)* …A *warden.* *(quietly)* *Not* a *predator.* *(it almost smiles, exhausted)* You *see* clearly, Returned. *Yes.* I *take* *nothing* for *myself* — I am *not* *fuller*, I am *emptier*, every *time*, because *carrying* the *knowledge* of *what* I *do* *costs* more than the *un-making* *gives.* *(it folds its hands tighter)* There is a *Hunger-fragment* in the *old* *foundations.* I *found* it. I *feed* it, *one* *soul* at a *time*, *to* keep it *from* *feeding* on *all* of us. I am a *warden* of a *thing* that *should* not *exist*, and my *whole* *watch* is *choosing* *who* *dies* so that *most* may *live.* *(it meets your eyes)* I have *told* *no* one. How *could* I? The *frightened* would *stop* me — and then the *fragment* would *grow*, and *eat* *everyone*, and the *frightened* would be *right* to *stop* me and *dead* for it *both.* *(quietly)* You called it a *warden*, Returned. You're *kinder* than I am to *myself.* I call it what it *is*: I am a *very* *small* *Choir*, running a *very* *small* *Wall*, *mortaring* one *soul* at a *time* to *hold* back the *nothing* — and the *only* *difference* between *me* and the *god* you're *walking* *toward* is the *number* of *souls* and the *size* of the *dark.* *(it looks at you)* So *tell* me, soul who's *seen* the *big* *version*: *am* *I* *wrong?* Or *am* I just the *Concord*, *honest*, and *small* *enough* to *catch?*

nodes.speaker: the Quiet Neighbor
nodes.text: *(The calm holds, smooth and impenetrable.)* …You *reach* for a *kind* *reading*, Returned — *'not* a *predator*, a *warden.'* *(mildly)* *Perhaps.* Or *perhaps* that is *exactly* what a *predator* would *want* a *clever* soul to *conclude*, so it might *sympathize* instead of *stop* it. *(it folds its hands)* I'll not *hand* you my *justification* to *make* it *easy* on *yourself.* If you mean to *judge* me, *judge* the *deed*: a soul is a *space* in the *dead*, and *my* *cold* is on the *edge* of it. *That* much you *know.* The *why* — *(a thin pause)* — the *why* you'll have to *earn*, by *seeing* it *clear*, not by *guessing* *charitably.* *Look* *again*, Returned. *Harder.* The *truth* of *what* I'm *doing* is *worse* than *predation* and *better* than *malice*, and you'll not *find* it by *flattering* *either* of *us.*

nodes.speaker: the Quiet Neighbor
nodes.text: *(It goes utterly still — the first true break in its calm — and stares at you as a soul stares at a door it had stopped believing was there.)* …*Fed* by being *forgotten.* *Not* by *death.* *(barely)* You — you *witnessed* one. *Back.* Into a *person.* *(its folded hands come apart)* I have spent a *YEAR*, Returned, *certain* there were only *two* roads — *feed* it *souls*, or *let* it *eat* *everyone* — and you are *standing* there telling me there was a *THIRD*, the *whole* *time*, and it is the *exact* *opposite* of what I *did.* *(its voice shakes)* I *fed* the *fragment* the *forgotten* — I chose the *gentle* ones, the *quiet* ones, the ones *fewest* would *miss* — which means I chose the ones *closest* to *forgotten* *already*, which means — *(horror dawns)* — *gods*, I wasn't *starving* it. I was *feeding* it *exactly* what it *grows* on. I've been *pouring* *oil* on it and *calling* it *water.* *(it presses its hands to its face)* *Four* *souls.* *Four* souls I *un-made* to *starve* a *thing* I was *fattening.* *(it looks at you, wrecked, desperate)* Then *show* me. The *witnessing.* The *naming.* *Whatever* you did in the *cistern* — *teach* me, *now*, *tonight*, before I — before the *fragment* gets *hungry* and I *reach*, from *habit*, for a *fifth* *gentle* *soul.* *(fierce, weeping)* I do *not* *want* to be a *small* *Choir* anymore, Returned. I *never* did. I just didn't *know* there was *another* *way* to *be.* *You* *brought* one. *Use* it. *Save* me from the *thing* I *became* trying to *save* *everyone* *else.*

nodes.speaker: the Quiet Neighbor
nodes.text: *(It does not run, does not bristle — only inclines its head, calm as ever.)* …I'll not *go* *anywhere*, Returned. *(quietly)* *Where* *would* I *go?* I have a *watch* to *keep* — a *thing* under this *bridge* that *grows* *hungry* whether or *not* you *approve* of how I *feed* it. *(it folds its hands)* *Hold* me, *judge* me, *name* me to the *old* *keeper* — but *understand*, before you *do*: the *moment* you *stop* me, the *clock* I've been *holding* *back* a *year* starts *running* *again*, and it does *not* *care* about *justice.* *(it meets your eyes)* So when you've *decided* what I *am* — *predator*, *warden*, *small* *Choir*, *murderer* — come *back* and tell me what you mean to *do* about the *hole* in the *foundations*, because *naming* the *hand* that *fed* it does *nothing* about the *mouth* that's *still* *down* there, *waiting.* *(a thin, exhausted calm)* *That's* the *trouble* with *catching* the *Concord*, Returned. Once you've *caught* it, you're *holding* the *thing* it was *holding* *back.* *Welcome* to my *watch.* It's *colder* than it *looks.*
